$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; queryId + artisanpack/postPreview added to usesContext so the editor preview can read the query-loop context. attribute schema and supports carried verbatim for parity.

Previews via the lightweight createEntityPlaceholderEdit helper. The adjacent-post identity is a derived sibling-of-current-post relation the server computes at render time, so the editor preview synthesizes a styled fallback from the block's label / arrow / type attributes instead of querying core-data. Front-end markup is produced server-side from the stamped _resolvedAdjacent* attributes.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from stamped _resolvedAdjacent* attributes.
Bidirectional core/post-navigation-link ↔ artisanpack/post-navigation-link block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

Single v1 entry ported to TypeScript: migrates the legacy top-level textAlign attribute into the canonical block-support shape via the vendored migrate-text-align helper. Save returns null — block is server-rendered.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit rather than upstream edit.js — the adjacent-post identity isn't computable in the editor without a network round-trip the upstream edit also lacks (upstream just renders the configured label or a placeholder).
post-navigation-link is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolvedAdjacent* attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/post-navigation-link during the V1 rollout.
